calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-03-2025 Continued To Jun-24-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice And Motion To Lift Arbitration Stay, Withdraw From Arbitration, And Request For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 1281.97-1281.99
Off Calendar Sep-11-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jessica Sanderson, Esq,. of TERMECHI EMPLOYMENT LAW, Tel:(310) 974-3324 for plaintiff (via Zoom Conference)
Mark Wilson, Esq., of MANNING & KASS,ELLROD, RAMIREZ, TRESTER LLP., Tel: 415-217-6990 for defendant.

Hearing was held.A timely Notice of Contest was received from Plaintiff's counsel regaridng the entirety of the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's counsel stated that the arbitration process has not yet occured but is in the process of being initiated. The delay was due to a failure to timely pay the required fee deposit for the arbitrator's compensation. When the matter was again brought to counsel's attention, counsel promptly submitted payment. Plaintiff Counsel further represented that arbitration dates have already been received; however, Defendant's counsel indicated they have not been informed of any such dates. The Court allowed Plaintiff's counsel time to locate and produce the relevant email communications. After a reasonable period, Plaintiff's counsel was unable to provide the referenced email. The matter argued/discussed. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF VICTOR ROMERO'S Motion To Lift Arbitration Stay, Withdraw From Arbitration, And Request For Sanctions Pursuant To CCP 1281.97-1281.99. is taken under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***An ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO LIFT ARBITRATION STAY, WITHDRAW FROM ARBITRATION, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS signed after hearing.***

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-24-2025 Continued To Nov-19-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Jan-15-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SBM MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LP's MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to compel arbitration is granted. The entire action is stayed pending conclusion of the arbitration (see word minutes for complete ruling).

Order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/HEK)
